servinfo.title: P/S System Check

attention1.ptxt: Carry out test drive in light traffic area to prevent an accident.

servinfo.ptxt: Refer to the following items for the details of each step.

action.ptxt: Print DTC or write it down and clear it by referring to DTC Clearance , and then go to Step 3.
Repair or replace malfunction part(s) and then go to Step 11.
Repair or replace malfunction part(s) and then go to Step 11.

testgroup.title: Steering symptom diagnosis and P/S system symptom diagnosis
testgroup.question: Are check and repair complete?

action.ptxt: Check and repair malfunction part(s) and then go to Step 10.
Check and repair malfunction part(s) and then go to Step 11.
Repair or replace malfunction part(s) and then go to Step 11.

testgroup.title: Final confirmation test
testgroup.question: Is there any problem symptom, DTC or abnormal condition?

servinfosub.title: Step 1: Customer Complaint Analysis
servinfosub.ptxt: Record details of the problem (failure, complaint) and how it occurred as described by the customer. For this purpose, use of such an inspection form as the following one will facilitate collecting information to the point required for proper analysis and diagnosis. Check if the problem described in “Customer questionnaire” occurs actually in the vehicle if necessary.
(This step should be performed with the customer if possible.)

attention3.ptxt: This form is a standard sample. It should be modified according to conditions characteristic of each market.

servinfosub.title: Step 2: DTC Check, Record and Clearance
servinfosub.ptxt: Check DTC (current and pending DTC) refer to DTC Check .
If DTC is indicated, print or write down the DTC and then clear the DTC referring to DTC Clearance .

servinfosub.title: Step 3 and 4: Visual Inspection
servinfosub.ptxt: Visually check “P/S” warning light for proper operation, connectors for proper condition and each part condition referring to Visual Inspection .

servinfosub.title: Step 5: Trouble Symptom Confirmation
servinfosub.ptxt: Based on Step 1: Customer Complaint Analysis and Step 2: DTC Check, Record and Clearance , confirm trouble symptoms. Also, reconfirm trouble symptoms by performing test drive and turning steering wheel fully to right and left with vehicle stopped.

servinfosub.title: Step 6 and 7: Rechecking and Record of DTC
servinfosub.ptxt: Refer to DTC Check for checking procedure.

servinfosub.title: Step 8: Steering Symptom Diagnosis and P/S System Symptom Diagnosis
servinfosub.ptxt:
Perform basic steering system check first.
When the end of the flow has been reached, check the parts of the system suspected as a possible cause referring to P/S System Symptom Diagnosis and based on symptoms appearing on the vehicle (symptoms obtained through steps of customer complaint analysis, trouble symptom confirmation and/or basic P/S system check). Repair or replace faulty parts.

servinfosub.title: Step 9: Troubleshooting for DTC
servinfosub.ptxt: Perform applicable DTC troubleshooting and repair or replace faulty parts.

servinfosub.title: Step 10: Intermittent Problems Check
servinfosub.ptxt: Check parts where an intermittent trouble is easy to occur (e.g., wire harness, connector, etc.), referring to Intermittent Connection and Poor Contact Inspection and related circuit of DTC recorded in Step 2.

servinfosub.title: Step 11: Final Confirmation Test
servinfosub.ptxt: Confirm that the problem symptom has disappeared and the P/S system is free from any abnormal conditions. If what has been repaired is related to the DTC, clear the DTC once, perform DTC confirmation procedure and confirm that no DTC is indicated.
